channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump's team says 'no children' died from USAID cuts. Consider these 3 cases
item.description: Both Marco Rubio and Elon Musk, who led the effort to sunset the foreign aid agency, have said that no deaths have been linked to the cutting of its funding. These parents tell a different story.
item.content:encoded:
Both Marco Rubio and Elon Musk, who led the effort to sunset the foreign aid agency, have said that no deaths have been linked to the cutting of its funding. These parents tell a different story.

(Image credit: From left: David Augustine, Lameck Nyagudi and Kazeem Olawale Nasiru for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: FDA approves new cholesterol-lowering drug
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration has approved a powerful new kind of cholesterol-lowering drug.
item.content:encoded: The Food and Drug Administration has approved a powerful new kind of cholesterol-lowering drug.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Testing service members' testosterone levels is a tricky proposition
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Dr. Adriane Fugh-Berman of Georgetown University about the Defense Department's plan to screen service members' testosterone levels and offer supplementation.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Dr. Adriane Fugh-Berman of Georgetown University about the Defense Department's plan to screen service members' testosterone levels and offer supplementation.
item.dc:creator: Vincent Acovino

item.title: TrumpRx promised a supermarket for cheaper drugs but delivered a boutique
item.description: President Trump said TrumpRx is the biggest thing to happen in healthcare in decades. But an NPR analysis finds drugmakers are only offering deals on a few of their medicines.
item.content:encoded: President Trump said TrumpRx is the biggest thing to happen in healthcare in decades. But an NPR analysis finds drugmakers are only offering deals on a few of their medicines.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin

item.title: CDC director nominee Erica Schwartz faced questions in Senate hearing
item.description: Dr. Erica Schwartz answered questions from a Senate committee Wednesday, as she sought confirmation to become the next director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.
item.content:encoded: Dr. Erica Schwartz answered questions from a Senate committee Wednesday, as she sought confirmation to become the next director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Cancer disparities researchers say federal funding changes have disrupted their work
item.description: In a survey, 93% of cancer researchers who study disparities said federal policy changes have affected them. Funding is harder to come by and they worry it's slowing progress in their field.
item.content:encoded:
In a survey, 93% of cancer researchers who study disparities said federal policy changes have affected them. Funding is harder to come by and they worry it's slowing progress in their field.

(Image credit: Andrew Brookes/Image Source)
item.dc:creator: Joseph Kim

item.title: How to keep cool in this year's extreme summer heat even without air conditioning
item.description: "Extreme heat" is in the forecast this summer. How do people cope if they don't have air conditioning? Here are suggestions from a heat researcher who grew up in a very hot, AC-less place.
item.content:encoded: "Extreme heat" is in the forecast this summer. How do people cope if they don't have air conditioning? Here are suggestions from a heat researcher who grew up in a very hot, AC-less place.
item.dc:creator: Gulrez Shah Azhar

item.title: CDC director nominee Erica Schwartz faced questions in Senate hearing
item.description: Trump's nominee faces the Senate health committee for her confirmation hearing on July 15. If confirmed, she will lead an agency dealing with workforce and leadership shortages and new layers of political review.
item.content:encoded:
Trump's nominee faces the Senate health committee for her confirmation hearing on July 15. If confirmed, she will lead an agency dealing with workforce and leadership shortages and new layers of political review.

(Image credit: Finn Gomez)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: More than 1,600 cases of intestinal illness cyclosporiasis reported in the U.S.
item.description: The CDC has confirmed more than 1,600 cases of a gastrointestinal parasitic infection. More than 140 people have been hospitalized, as the CDC looks for the source.
item.content:encoded: The CDC has confirmed more than 1,600 cases of a gastrointestinal parasitic infection. More than 140 people have been hospitalized, as the CDC looks for the source.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Wheelchair users say lack of competition in repair market leads to long wait times
item.description: People who use wheelchairs say simple repairs can take months to complete and replacement parts can be very expensive. In part, they blame two private equity firms that control most of the market.
item.content:encoded: People who use wheelchairs say simple repairs can take months to complete and replacement parts can be very expensive. In part, they blame two private equity firms that control most of the market.
item.dc:creator: Meghan H Smith
